Danish slave trade development

2026-05-21

First I activate the libraries

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(gganimate)
library(gifski)
library(dplyr)

Installing and renaming the data

read_csv("taden.csv")
## Rows: 412 Columns: 46
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (25): Name of vessel, Outcome of voyage if ship captured, Flag of vessel...
## dbl (21): Voyage ID, Year arrived with captives, Total disembarked (IMP), To...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## # A tibble: 412 × 46
##    `Voyage ID` `Name of vessel`    Year arrived with ca…¹ Total disembarked (I…²
##          <dbl> <chr>                                <dbl>                  <dbl>
##  1         112 Maria                                 1820                    160
##  2        1603 Esperança                             1837                    304
##  3        1970 Margareth                             1839                    444
##  4        2182 0                                     1842                    400
##  5        2183 0                                     1842                    350
##  6        2217 0                                     1841                    350
##  7        2329 Joseph                                1822                    188
##  8        4270 Saint Olof                            1858                      0
##  9        7548 Diana                                 1810                     84
## 10       11385 S Jansburgh (a) Jo…                   1657                    259
## # ℹ 402 more rows
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Year arrived with captives`,
## #   ²​`Total disembarked (IMP)`
## # ℹ 42 more variables: `Total embarked (IMP)` <dbl>,
## #   `Duration of captives' crossing (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Voyage duration, homeport to disembarkation (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Crew at first landing of captives` <dbl>, `Crew at voyage outset` <dbl>, …
danish_slave_trade <- read_csv("taden.csv")
## Rows: 412 Columns: 46
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (25): Name of vessel, Outcome of voyage if ship captured, Flag of vessel...
## dbl (21): Voyage ID, Year arrived with captives, Total disembarked (IMP), To...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
danish_slave_trade
## # A tibble: 412 × 46
##    `Voyage ID` `Name of vessel`    Year arrived with ca…¹ Total disembarked (I…²
##          <dbl> <chr>                                <dbl>                  <dbl>
##  1         112 Maria                                 1820                    160
##  2        1603 Esperança                             1837                    304
##  3        1970 Margareth                             1839                    444
##  4        2182 0                                     1842                    400
##  5        2183 0                                     1842                    350
##  6        2217 0                                     1841                    350
##  7        2329 Joseph                                1822                    188
##  8        4270 Saint Olof                            1858                      0
##  9        7548 Diana                                 1810                     84
## 10       11385 S Jansburgh (a) Jo…                   1657                    259
## # ℹ 402 more rows
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Year arrived with captives`,
## #   ²​`Total disembarked (IMP)`
## # ℹ 42 more variables: `Total embarked (IMP)` <dbl>,
## #   `Duration of captives' crossing (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Voyage duration, homeport to disembarkation (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Crew at first landing of captives` <dbl>, `Crew at voyage outset` <dbl>, …
show_col_types = FALSE

Now I use head() and unique() to see if the data is correct and remove dublicants

head(danish_slave_trade)
## # A tibble: 6 × 46
##   `Voyage ID` `Name of vessel` Year arrived with captiv…¹ Total disembarked (I…²
##         <dbl> <chr>                                 <dbl>                  <dbl>
## 1         112 Maria                                  1820                    160
## 2        1603 Esperança                              1837                    304
## 3        1970 Margareth                              1839                    444
## 4        2182 0                                      1842                    400
## 5        2183 0                                      1842                    350
## 6        2217 0                                      1841                    350
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Year arrived with captives`,
## #   ²​`Total disembarked (IMP)`
## # ℹ 42 more variables: `Total embarked (IMP)` <dbl>,
## #   `Duration of captives' crossing (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Voyage duration, homeport to disembarkation (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Crew at first landing of captives` <dbl>, `Crew at voyage outset` <dbl>,
## #   `Standardized Tonnage (IMP)` <dbl>, …
unique(danish_slave_trade)
## # A tibble: 412 × 46
##    `Voyage ID` `Name of vessel`    Year arrived with ca…¹ Total disembarked (I…²
##          <dbl> <chr>                                <dbl>                  <dbl>
##  1         112 Maria                                 1820                    160
##  2        1603 Esperança                             1837                    304
##  3        1970 Margareth                             1839                    444
##  4        2182 0                                     1842                    400
##  5        2183 0                                     1842                    350
##  6        2217 0                                     1841                    350
##  7        2329 Joseph                                1822                    188
##  8        4270 Saint Olof                            1858                      0
##  9        7548 Diana                                 1810                     84
## 10       11385 S Jansburgh (a) Jo…                   1657                    259
## # ℹ 402 more rows
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Year arrived with captives`,
## #   ²​`Total disembarked (IMP)`
## # ℹ 42 more variables: `Total embarked (IMP)` <dbl>,
## #   `Duration of captives' crossing (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Voyage duration, homeport to disembarkation (in days)` <dbl>,
## #   `Crew at first landing of captives` <dbl>, `Crew at voyage outset` <dbl>, …
theme_set(theme_bw(base_family = "times new roman"))
options(scipen=999)
view(danish_slave_trade)

unique(danish_slave_trade$`Name of vessel`)

unique(danish_slave_trade$`Year arrived with captives`)
##   [1] 1820 1837 1839 1842 1841 1822 1858 1810 1657 1798 1799 1641 1660 1797 1802
##  [16] 1803 1804 1805 1696 1679 1686 1687 1688 1690 1691 1692 1693 1694 1695 1699
##  [31] 1701 1702 1703 1715 1683 1646 1760 1770 1801 1806 1807 1796 1795 1778 1779
##  [46] 1780 1781 1782 1783 1784 1785 1786 1787 1788 1789 1705 1698 1700 1704 1707
##  [61] 1709 1714 1718 1722 1724 1727 1728 1729 1731 1732 1733 1734 1736 1739 1740
##  [76] 1742 1744 1746 1747 1748 1749 1750 1751 1752 1753 1726 1710 1754 1755 1757
##  [91] 1762 1764 1758 1759 1761 1767 1768 1769 1772 1775 1776 1774 1790 1791 1793
## [106] 1794 1800 1756 1765 1771 1773 1647 1823 1606 1847
danish_slave_trade <- danish_slave_trade %>%
  rename(
    vessel = `Name of vessel`,
    year = `Year arrived with captives`
  )

Converting the data into numeric and removing NA’s

danish_slave_trade <- danish_slave_trade %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year))

data_filtered <- danish_slave_trade %>%
  filter(year >= 1606, year <= 1805)

ships_per_year <- danish_slave_trade %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(ships = n())

ships_per_year
## # A tibble: 115 × 2
##     year ships
##    <dbl> <int>
##  1  1606     1
##  2  1641     1
##  3  1646     2
##  4  1647     2
##  5  1657     1
##  6  1660     1
##  7  1679     1
##  8  1683     1
##  9  1686     1
## 10  1687     3
## # ℹ 105 more rows

Graph - Danish slave trade development

ggplot(ships_per_year, aes(x = year, y = ships)) +
  geom_line(color = "lightpink") + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(ships_per_year$year),
                                  max(ships_per_year$year),
                                  by = 50)) +
  labs(
    title = "Danish slave trade development",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of ships"
  )

Animation - Danish slave trade development

anim <- ggplot(ships_per_year, aes(x = year, y = ships)) +
  geom_line(color = "lightpink") +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Year {round(frame_along)}",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of ships"
  ) +
  transition_reveal(year)

anim
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

Graph - Slaves dying during crossing over the years

death <- danish_slave_trade %>%
  select(year, `Total disembarked (IMP)`, `Total embarked (IMP)`) %>%
  mutate(death = `Total embarked (IMP)` - `Total disembarked (IMP)`)

ggplot(death, aes(
  x = year,
  y = death
)) +
  geom_line(color = "lightpink", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Deaths under the Danish slave trade",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of people who died during crossing"
  ) +
  theme_classic()+
  theme_set(theme_bw(base_family = "times new roman"))+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1600, 1860, by = 25))+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 859, by = 50))
